heaviest_rainfall.status: On 09 November 2025, a record rainfall of 47.89mm was measured, indicating an exceptionally wet day.  24.6mm per hour was recorded as falling at times during the 144.8 hour storm.

heaviest_rainrate.status: On 02 September 2025, the rain fell at an exceptional rate, peaking at 34.3mm per hour, the heaviest downpour recorded.  A total of 21.19mm fell over the 15.0 hour storm.

longest_rain.status: The record duration for continuous rain was set on 09 November 2025, the storm lasted 144.8 hours with a total precipitation of 47.89mm and it reached 24.6mm per hour at times.

most_lightning.status: The highest recorded number of lightning strikes in a single day is {highest_count} on {highest_date} since records began on 11 April 2025.

storm.status: Stormy conditions are expected.  This is the first storm in the region since 28 January 2026.

top_10_dry.status: The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: It is currently one of the longest rainy periods.  It is the ranked 1st since our records started in 23 September 2024.  There have been 18 consecutive days where it has been rainy starting on 13 January 2026.

cold_snaps.status: There is no cold snap at the moment.  This year has experienced 3 cold snaps, with an average of 3.7 per year.

extreme.status: The largest temperature gap recorded is 26.7°C (0.4 - 27.1°C) on 29 April 2025.

heatwaves.status: There is no heatwaves at the moment.  This year has experienced 0 heatwavess, with an average of 1.9 per year.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was on 18 July 2025 and it has only happened 62 times since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (202°).  In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: The largest wind gust ever was on 27 January 2026 with a value of 128.2 mph.

run.status: The highest wind run ever seen is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The record wind speed remains at 93.7 mph from 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the inside temperature (down 2.7°C) and more moisture-laden rainfall (up 3.1 mm).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in cloud levels (up 146 m).

The past week and month had observable variation in cloud levels (up 312 m), rainfall (down 2.4 mm), and inside temperature (up 3.7°C) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 146.5m lower than yesterday, 230.7m lower than the past week, 312.3m lower than the past month.

The cloud levels yesterday fell between 524m minimum and 1035m maximum, with an average of 548.7m; weekly and monthly averages were marginally higher at 779.4m and 861.0m.

summary.summary: Inside; on the cooler side. Outside; cold spells are likely. Also, dry and less humid conditions and calm and still conditions are expected. Some rain can be expected, fog or low cloud is expected, and the light is absent. Weather could become unsettled.
